packet.criterion_text: Objective severity trend: PASI, BSA, and/or IGA show a directional change across visits.

evidence.quote: Severity scoring today: PASI 6.1, BSA 8%, IGA 2
PASI 6.3 today (6.1 on 2026-02-02 — essentially flat, within measurement noise). BSA 8%. IGA 2.
Few scattered erythematous papules over the antecubital arm, limited extent
Confluent erythematous plaques with thick scale over extensor arm, extensive severe

packet.reasoning: PASI is essentially flat from 2026-02-02 (6.1) to 2026-03-16 (6.3), then rises sharply to 14.2 on 2026-05-10 and 16.8 on 2026-06-29 — increases well beyond the >=2-point threshold. BSA climbs 8%->15%->18% and IGA steps up from 2 to 3. All four visits document objective scores, so the worsening trend is well supported. Photo progression from limited papules to extensive severe plaques corroborates the numeric trend.

packet.criterion_text: Symptom trajectory, including nail and joint involvement, shows a directional change.

evidence.quote: onycholysis noted on two fingernails (right index and right middle), with distal separation and slight yellowish discoloration
Nail plate with longitudinal ridging and mild dystrophy, subtle discoloration
Erythematous scaly plaque at frontal scalp margin with overlying hair, localized
Thick scaly erythematous plaques over frontal scalp with adherent scale, extensive

packet.reasoning: New nail involvement (onycholysis of two fingernails) appears at 2026-05-10, corroborated by the nail photo showing ridging and dystrophy — a new finding not present at earlier visits. Scalp involvement, localized at 2026-05-10, progresses to extensive thick plaques by 2026-06-29. This reflects progressing symptoms across new anatomic sites. No joint symptoms are documented in the candidates.
packet.gap: No structured symptom or nail/joint review is recorded at the February or March visits, so the nail and scalp findings can only be timed from the May visit onward.

packet.criterion_text: DLQI (Dermatology Life Quality Index) trend shows a directional change.

evidence.quote: Quality-of-life impact: DLQI 7, driven by residual itch and self-consciousness at the gym.
DLQI 15 today (was 8), with high scores on symptoms, feelings, and work domains.

packet.reasoning: DLQI is stable early (7 at 2026-02-02, 8 at 2026-03-16), then jumps to 15 at 2026-05-10 and 17 at 2026-06-29. The rise from 8 to 15 far exceeds the >=4-point threshold, and the sustained upward trend to 17 confirms worsening quality-of-life impact tracking the objective severity increase.

packet.criterion_text: Treatment-response adequacy: the current regimen has been given an adequate, adherent trial.

evidence.quote: Adherence and pharmacy fill detail deferred to today's separate review by clinic staff (filed separately).

packet.reasoning: The candidates do not identify the current regimen, its start date, or dosing schedule, and the only adherence-relevant quote explicitly defers pharmacy fill and adherence detail to a separate document that is not among the candidates. Without regimen identity, duration, and confirmed dosing, adequacy of an adherent trial cannot be judged.
packet.gap: The chart never states which medication the patient is on, when it started, or whether doses were actually taken — the adherence review is filed separately and not available here.

packet.criterion_text: Worsening cannot be attributed to disease if a confounder (non-adherence, infection, steroid-withdrawal or seasonal flare) is unaddressed.
packet.reasoning: No candidate evidence speaks to any confounder class — there are no quotes addressing adherence status, intercurrent infection, steroid taper/withdrawal, or seasonal pattern. With no chart material to either exclude a confounder or establish that worsening preceded one, the attribution question cannot be assembled from the available evidence.
packet.gap: Nothing in the available documents addresses whether missed medications, infection, a steroid taper, or a seasonal flare could explain the worsening, so those alternative explanations remain unexamined.

attempts: confounder missed doses infection steroid seasonal
